meta.description: Position sizing, stop loss / take profit rules, and account protection limits attached to every signal.

coreRules.dailyLossLimit: 3% — stop trading for the day when hit.
coreRules.weeklyLossLimit: 7% — stop trading for the week when hit.
neverRules: Never martingale or increase size after a loss.
Never move a stop loss further away.
Never trade without a stop loss.
Never risk more to 'win it back'.

positionSizing.example: Balance $500, risk 1% ($5), SL 250 points on V75 at $0.01/point per 0.001 lot -> size = 5 / (250 x 0.01) x 0.001-scaled.
steps: 1. Determine SL distance from STRUCTURE first (never from desired lot size).
2. Compute risk amount = balance x riskPercent.
3. Solve for lot size. Round DOWN to broker minimum increments.
4. If computed size is below broker minimum, skip the trade — do not widen risk.

stopLossRules: SL goes beyond the structure that invalidates the idea (swing point, zone edge, sweep extreme).
Add a volatility buffer: 0.5 x ATR(14) of the entry timeframe.
Boom/Crash: SL must survive one adverse spike if holding counter-drift is even possible — otherwise don't take the trade.
Time stop: exit scalps that go nowhere within 10 entry-TF candles.
takeProfitRules: TP1 at the nearest opposing structure/liquidity (bank 50%, SL to breakeven).
TP2 at fib extension 1.272-1.618 or the HTF POI.
Trend trades may trail remainder by structure (below each new HL / above each new LH).
Minimum R:R: scalps 1:1.5, trend setups 1:2, APA reversals 1:3.

riskRewardTable.note: Lower required win rates at higher R:R are why patience for quality setups beats overtrading.

drawdownProtocol: Down 3% in a day -> stop for the day.
Down 5% in a week -> half size for the rest of the week.
Down 10% overall -> paper trade until 10 consecutive rule-perfect sessions.
After any SL widening or revenge trade -> mandatory journal review before next session.
syntheticsSpecific: V75/V100 stops are wide in points; size DOWN accordingly — same dollar risk, fewer lots.
Boom/Crash sells (Boom) and buys (Crash) carry asymmetric spike risk: cap size at half normal.
24/7 market: define session start/end times and respect them; fatigue is a risk parameter.
